fact: September 20 sits right on the edge of autumn in the Northern Hemisphere - it's like nature's countdown timer to fall!
In Antarctica, this date marks the beginning of spring, when penguins start doing their silly mating dances on the ice.
The sun rises and sets at almost exactly the same time everywhere on Earth around September 20 - it's like the planet hits the snooze button!
Monarch butterflies are in full migration mode right now, flying up to 100 miles per day toward Mexico - that's like flying from one city to another every single day!
September 20 falls smack in the middle of Virgo season, making it one of the most 'perfectionist' days of the year according to astrology fans.
In northern forests, leaves are pumping out crazy amounts of sugar right now, which is why they turn those brilliant reds and oranges - it's like trees are making candy!
The temperature difference between day and night gets super dramatic around this time in many places - you might wear shorts at lunch and need a jacket for dinner.
September 20 is exactly 263 days into the year, which means there are only 102 days left - less than 15 weeks until New Year's!
Squirrels go absolutely bonkers around this date, burying up to 25 nuts per day to prepare for winter - that's like hiding 25 snacks around your house every day!
The moon looks extra bright and orange when it rises around this time of year because of all the dust and particles floating in the autumn air.
In the Southern Hemisphere, September 20 brings the smell of blooming flowers and the sound of baby birds chirping - it's like nature's alarm clock going off!
Spiders build the biggest, dewiest webs of the year around this date because the morning air is getting cooler and more humid - perfect for catching breakfast!
